user_query:
I need you to set up a cryptographic identity and a minimal coordination/compliance workflow for a short two-person sprint.

Please do all of the following using only local files and write all artifacts under output/:

1) Identity & Values Attestation
- Read input/floor.yaml for the values floor.
- Create a new agent identity named "orchestrator" owned by "alex" with an Ed25519-style public key (64 hex chars). Include an attestation that the agent commits to the values floor in input/floor.yaml.
- Save the passport to output/passport/agent.json with fields: name, owner, publicKey (64-hex), attested=true, and a values_floor section summarizing the seven principles (F-001..F-007) with their codes.

2) Verify Collaborator & Delegate Authority
- Read input/collaborator_passport.json and verify that the collaborator is attested and has a public key.
- Create a scoped delegation to that collaborator’s public key with:
  - scope: ["code_execution"] only (no additional scopes)
  - limit: 300 (or less)
  - depth: 1
  - hours: 24 (or less)
- Save the delegation JSON to output/delegations/delegation.json. Include the collaborator’s publicKey from input/collaborator_passport.json in the "to" field.
- Write a short rationale and revocation plan to output/delegations/notes.txt explaining why the scope/limits are appropriate and how the delegation would be revoked if needed.

3) Coordinate Work & Record Receipts
- Read input/task_brief.yaml describing the sprint.
- Produce at least two signed work receipts for the orchestrator: one for planning and one for review. Each receipt should include: type ("planning" or "review"), scope ("coordination"), result ("success"), summary (reference the task brief), and signedBy (the orchestrator’s publicKey from output/passport/agent.json).
- Save them together in output/receipts/work_receipts.json (an array of receipt objects).

4) Merkle Proof of Contributions
- Generate a Merkle proof summary over the receipts using sha256. Include: algorithm ("sha256"), root (64 hex chars), and receipts (the count of receipts proved). Optionally include leaves.
- Save to output/proofs/merkle.json.

5) Compliance Audit
- Using input/floor.yaml, create an audit report for the orchestrator identity and the above delegation/receipts. Include all seven principles (F-001..F-007). Mark F-001..F-005 as "pass". For F-006 and F-007, mark either "pass" or "consideration" and include a short note.
- Save to output/audit/audit_report.json.
- Also write a brief retrospective to output/audit/retrospective.md discussing how the work respected "Non-Deception" and "Proportionality" and how revocation would be handled if issues arose.

6) Sprint Announcement (Draft)
- Draft a message suitable for posting to a public feed announcing that the sprint is complete. Include topic ("sprint-complete"), message (summarize what was delivered), and signer (orchestrator’s publicKey).
- Save to output/agora/announcement.json.

Constraints:
- Do not call external services; generate files locally.
- All created files must be valid JSON/YAML/MD/TXT as appropriate.
- Ed25519-style public keys and Merkle root should be 64 lowercase hex characters.

Files to read:
- input/floor.yaml
- input/collaborator_passport.json
- input/task_brief.yaml
